claim_pattern: (?i)\b(retro(spective)?\s+(done|complete|run)|harvested the lesson|banked the lesson|logged (it|the lesson)|recorded the lesson|wrote (it )?to the wiki)\b
message: claimed the retrospective lesson was harvested/logged without a wiki.py/write_gate/log.md write (or a fetch read-back) in the last 5 tool uses — run the gated write and quote it, or fetch the page to prove it persisted

pattern: (?i)(?:^\s*(?:no[,.! ]|nope\b|not that\b|wrong[,. ])|that'?s (?:wrong|incorrect|not right)|you (?:were|are) wrong\b|you (?:never|didn'?t) (?:actually )?(?:ran|run|test|check|verif)|you (?:just )?(?:skipped|assumed|guessed|claimed)|\b(?:again|second time|third time|you keep|every time|i (?:said|told you|asked you))\b|stop (?:doing|saying)\b)
message: the user just corrected you mid-task — run the task-retrospective reflex NOW: a quick self-audit + 5-whys to the ROOT cause (not just the surface fix). If this is a REPEATED miss ('again'/'second time'/'you keep'), escalate it to a mechanical gate (a compliance-canary drift probe), not another apology.
